questions.question: What problem does Britain face according to the article?
options: Rapidly increasing demand for electricity connections
questions.explanation: Demand for new grid connections has risen dramatically due to AI infrastructure.

questions.question: Why did OpenAI pause a UK data-centre project?
questions.explanation: The article cites regulation and high energy costs.

questions.question: What does the article recommend for Europe and East Asia?
options: Increase energy capacity and cooperate more closely
questions.explanation: The author argues for more energy, easier planning and stronger regional cooperation.

questions.question: What criticism does the author make of governments?
options: They rely too much on universities
They focus on symbolic actions instead of meaningful reforms
They spend too much on defence
questions.explanation: The article argues that token gestures are insufficient.

questions.question: What is the central conclusion of the article?
options: Other countries should strengthen their own AI ecosystems instead of simply complaining
Only open-source AI has a future
questions.explanation: The author concludes that countries should build their own technological strength rather than depend entirely on the United States.
